CCZeq¶

Given two functions in univariate form, returns true if they are CCZ-equivalent and false otherwise

 1function CCZeq(f1,f2)
 2   R:=Parent(f1);
 3   F:=BaseRing(R);
 4   n:=Degree(F); p:=Characteristic(F);
 5   V,phi:=VectorSpace(GF(p^n),GF(p));
 6   M:=KMatrixSpace(GF(p),2*n+1,p^n);
 7
 8f:=function(x)
 9   return Evaluate(f1,x); end function;
10
11g:=function(x)
12   return Evaluate(f2,x); end function;
13
14T:=M!0; i:=0;
15for x in GF(p^n) do
16i:=i+1;
17for j in [1..n] do
18T[j,i]:=phi(x)[j];
19T[j+n,i]:=phi(g(x))[j];
20end for;
21T[2*n+1,i]:=1;
22end for;
23C:=LinearCode(T);
24T:=M!0; i:=0;
25for x in GF(p^n) do
26i:=i+1;
27for j in [1..n] do
28T[j,i]:=phi(x)[j];
29T[j+n,i]:=phi(f(x))[j];
30end for;
31T[2*n+1,i]:=1;
32end for;
33D:=LinearCode(T);
34return IsEquivalent(C,D);
35end function;
